Inclusion Support

ERASMUS+ Inclusion and Diversity Strategy

The Erasmus+ program aims to ensure equal opportunities, access, inclusion, diversity, and fairness in all its activities. In this context, ensuring the equal participation of individuals with various barriers (social, economic, physical, psychological, etc.) in projects is of particular importance.

Erasmus+ Inclusion and Diversity Support

Participants with special needs are individuals who cannot participate in the program without additional financial support. Support may include:

Assistance      for physical, mental, or health needs (accessible accommodation, travel      assistance, medical support, suitable materials, or accompanying persons).

Additional      grants and supplementary support to enable participation.

Additional Grant

An additional grant may be provided for participants with special needs in the areas of physical or mental health. This grant may exceed standard grants to compensate for encountered difficulties and is based on actual costs.

Supplementary Grant Support

Disadvantaged participants may be awarded a supplementary grant in addition to their main grant. This includes:

Those entitled      to protection, care, or accommodation under Law No. 2828 or 5395.

Orphans or      children of martyrs or veterans.

Students      receiving a need-based allowance.

Persons with      disabilities (documented with a 70% or higher disability report).

Other      socially and economically disadvantaged categories.

Grant Amounts:

Type of    Mobility

Additional    Grant

Student   mobility (2–12 months)

€250   per month

Short-term   (5–14 days)

+€100   added to total daily grant

Short-term   (15–30 days)

+€150   added to total daily grant

Note:

Scholarships from the Student Credit and Dormitories Institution or merit-based scholarships are not included in this category.

How to Apply

Submit your Erasmus+ special needs support request to the

Istanbul Okan University Erasmus Institutional Coordination Office (Istanbul)

by mail. Applications are submitted together with the Office and evaluated by the

Turkish National Agency

.

Inclusion Officer

The Inclusion Officer ensures that participants with limited opportunities have equal access to projects under the Istanbul Okan University Erasmus+ Program.

Contact:
